title: Miso & Peanut Glazed Onions
description: Soft, deeply caramelised onions coated in a sticky miso-peanut glaze with a soy kick. A rich, umami-loaded side that transforms a simple bowl of rice, pasta or couscous.
steps: Peel and halve the onions, then slice them thinly into half-moons.
In a small bowl, whisk together the peanut butter, miso, soy sauce and warm water until you get a smooth, pourable sauce.

Heat the neutral oil and sesame oil together in a wide pan over medium heat. Add the onions and cook for 20-25 minutes, stirring regularly, until deeply golden and very soft. Add the sliced garlic for the last 5 minutes.
Pour the miso-peanut sauce over the onions and stir to coat everything evenly. Cook for another 3-4 minutes on low heat, stirring, until the sauce is thick and glossy and clings to the onions.
Serve immediately over rice, pasta, soba noodles or couscous.
footnote: White miso gives a milder, sweeter result; red or dark miso is more intense. The sauce thickens quickly once it hits the hot pan — keep stirring and add a splash of water if it catches.
